rag_rules: AGREE diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers or type mismatches should be resolved by using only visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex scope.
Repair clauses must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When fixing AGREE contract issues, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current component scope and match expected types.
AGREE annexes must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Avoid introducing new data types or property references that are not declared or imported in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
Helper declarations and expressions must use compatible scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not add new identifiers or types to AGREE contracts that are not already visible in the AADL component or its dependencies.
Do not attempt to resolve package or property references by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses using only existing visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
